title: Gently Springy Dutch Bottarga Pilaf
description: A romantic Dutch dish featuring bottarga, beurre-monte, and springy ingredients, prepared in a comal and suitable for an ayurvedic-sattvic diet.
instructions: Heat the comal over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Add the thinly sliced spring onions and cook until they are softened and lightly browned.
Add the basmati rice to the comal and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the grated bottarga, beurre-monte, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to the comal and stir well.
Reduce the heat to low and cover the comal with a lid.
Simmer the pilaf for 15 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Remove the lid and stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the Gently Springy Dutch Bottarga Pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
